sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 15.45 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.15)

statement.latex: We define the class of hierarchically decomposable\/ groups in the following way. First, if${ X}$is any class of groups, then let$H_1{ X}$denote the class of groups which admit an admissible action on a finite-dimensional contractible complex in such a way that every cell stabilizer belongs to${ X}$. Then the ``big'' class$H{ X}$is defined to be the smallest$H_1$-closed class containing${ X}$. [r]...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
